ORDER SUSTAINING DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT WITH LEAVE TO AMEND

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PETER BRESLER AND FAYE LEE'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST CLAIM. PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY PLEADS LIABILITY BASED ON DEFENDANT'S NEGLIGENT PREPARATION OF THE 2007 WILL AND TRUST. SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND CLAIM. DEFENDANT DID NOT OWE A DUTY OF CARE TO PLAINTIFF WITH REGARD TO THE PROPOSED AMENDMENT. LIABILITY ONLY ARISES AFTER THE TESTATOR EXECUTES THE DOCUMENTS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED

REPLY BRIEF FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AGAINST PLTF FILED BY DEFENDANT BRESLER, PETER H. LEE, FAYE

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PETER BRESLER AND FAYE LEE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED BECAUSE THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER DECEDENT PROVIDED INSTRUCTIONS TO DEFENDANT THAT ONLY THE PLAINTIFF AND NOT ANY FUTURE WIFE WOULD TAKE UNDER THE TESTAMENTARY INSTRUMENTS. THE COURT OVERRULES THE D'AMICO OBJECTION. PARAGRAPHS 5-6 OF PLAINTIFF'S DECLARATION AND PAGES 34:22-35:6 OF HER DEPOSITION ARE NOT NECESSARILY CONTRADICTORY. (THE COURT WILL MODIFY AND SIGN THE PROPOSED ORDER SUBMITTED BY THE PREVAILING PARTY BY EMAIL.) JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.
